learn.title: Floating Solar Farms on Water
learn.intro: Floating solar farms harness unused water bodies, conserving land and reducing evaporation. They offer sustainable energy solutions while addressing water scarcity in regions like India.
learn.c1_text: Floating solar reduces land use by 90% compared to ground-mounted systems. In India, this frees up 1.5 million hectares annually for agriculture or ecosystems, combating deforestation.
learn.c2_text: Water cooling lowers panel temperatures, boosting efficiency by 5-10%. A 2023 study found floating solar in Gujarat increased output by 8% compared to desert installations.
learn.c3_text: India’s 10 GW floating solar target by 2030 aims to save 12 billion liters of water yearly. Projects in Kerala and Tamil Nadu demonstrate this technology’s role in drought-prone regions.
learn.stat1: India: 10 GW floating solar target by 2030
learn.stat4: 12 billion liters water saved yearly in India

learn.title: Battery Management Systems (BMS)
learn.intro: BMS safeguards batteries from overcharging, overheating, and degradation, ensuring longevity and safety in EVs and grid storage systems.
learn.c1_text: BMS continuously tracks cell voltages, preventing 80% of battery failures caused by overcharge/over-discharge. Tesla’s BMS extends Model 3 battery life by 15 years.
learn.c2_text: Advanced BMS regulates temperature within ±5°C, critical for lithium-ion batteries. A 2022 study showed this reduces capacity loss by 30% in EVs like the Hyundai Kona.
learn.c3_text: India’s EV battery recycling policy mandates BMS integration, saving Rs 15,000 per kWh by extending battery life. BMS-enabled batteries in Delhi’s e-rickshaws last 10+ years.

learn.title: Capacity Factor Explained
learn.intro: Capacity factor measures how much a power plant produces versus its maximum potential. It reveals real-world efficiency gaps in renewable energy systems.
learn.c1_text: Capacity factor = (Actual Output / Max Potential Output) × 100. A 50% factor means a 100 MW plant generates 50 MW on average. Critical for grid planning.
learn.c2_text: Solar PV averages 20-25% capacity factor vs. 50-60% for gas plants. For example, India’s solar farms reach 22% due to seasonal monsoons.
learn.c3_text: India’s Bhadla Solar Park hit 28% capacity factor in 2023, outperforming global averages. This highlights India’s potential to optimize solar investments through advanced tracking systems.
learn.stat3: India’s Bhadla Solar Park: 28% capacity factor

learn.title: EV Charging Infrastructure
learn.intro: EV charging levels dictate speed and convenience. Understanding differences ensures optimal charging for daily use and long trips.
learn.c1_text: Uses standard 120V outlets, adding 2-5 miles per hour. Ideal for overnight home charging. 80% of U.S. EV owners use Level 1 at home.
learn.c2_text: Level 2 (240V) adds 10-20 miles/hour; DC fast chargers (480V+) add 75-200 miles in 30 mins. 70% of public chargers in India are DC fast.
learn.c3_text: India’s EV charging network expanded by 300% in 2023, with 12,000+ DC fast chargers. Policies like FAME II aim to add 500,000 chargers by 2025.

learn.title: Power Purchase Agreements (PPAs)
learn.intro: PPAs enable renewable projects to secure long-term contracts, reducing investment risks and accelerating clean energy adoption.
learn.c1_text: Developers sell future electricity at fixed prices, typically 20-30% below market rates. For example, India’s 2023 record solar PPA was ₹2.44/kWh.
learn.c2_text: PPAs transfer price volatility risks to buyers. In 2022, 65% of India’s 5 GW solar projects used PPAs to stabilize revenue.
learn.c3_text: India’s PPA market grew 40% in 2023, with 18 GW of solar/wind under contract. Tamil Nadu leads with 4 GW of PPA-backed renewables.
learn.stat2: 65% of India’s 2022 solar projects used PPAs

learn.title: Wind Turbine Lifespan & Recycling
learn.intro: Wind turbines last 20-25 years but face recycling challenges. Innovations in blade reuse and recycling are critical for sustainable wind energy growth.
learn.c1_text: Modern turbines last 20-25 years with minimal maintenance. India’s Kanyakumari Wind Farm reduced downtime by 15% using AI-driven predictive maintenance.
learn.c2_text: Most blades end in landfills. Vestas’ recyclable blades in India use thermoplastic resins, reducing waste by 90% compared to traditional epoxy blades.
learn.c3_text: India’s 2023 wind blade recycling policy mandates 50% material recovery. Companies like Suzlon recycle 30% of blades from decommissioned turbines in Tamil Nadu.
learn.stat4: India’s 30% blade recycling rate by Suzlon
